rationale: The cited paper is used in the monetary-incentives literature review to support a specific conceptual claim: loyalty/frequency program rewards tied to repeated effort can motivate continued engagement over time. This is not just a generic name-check; it directly underpins why the citing study expects monetary rewards to influence repeat usage and includes incentives as a core driver in its model. Although the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's method, it uses it as prior theoretical grounding for its incentive mechanism framing.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Kivetz and Simonson (2002) as conceptual prior work showing that effort-reward structures in loyalty programs can sustain repeated behavior. That evidence helps motivate and justify the citing paper's treatment of monetary incentives as a key determinant of repeat usage.
